refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource utilization.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation, task scheduling, resource utilization) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations; verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the productivity target.
sub_frs: FR2: Enable effective task scheduling and coordination

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation efficiency
DP2: Integrated teleoperation and task management system
DP3: Real-time resource monitoring and allocation services
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation efficiency

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and teleoperation modules
Coordination between task scheduling and resource monitoring
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from teleoperation and safety services but requires well-defined interfaces for telemetry and tasking.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate telemetry data is available for control algorithms
Risk of performance degradation if network link latency is high
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to reduce coupling and clarify interfaces.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle control and operation efficiency

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle control algorithms receive sensor data and send status updates via Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands and scheduling information from higher-level modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with teleoperation and safety services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of vehicle control logic; low diffusion as control algorithms are centralized.
Broader scope; control logic would be diluted if assigned here.
Not suitable; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not vehicle control algorithms.
No containment; safety services unrelated to vehicle control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the best architectural boundary to contain advanced vehicle control algorithms, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules
Risk of overloading Vehicle module if additional functionalities are added without clear boundaries
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider further modularization if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by focusing on vehicle operation efficiency, a key productivity driver.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if vehicle control algorithms are partially implemented outside Vehicle module
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment scores and appropriate interface assignments.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and monitoring telemetry data from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP2 and DP3 in next decomposition level to reduce coupling.
Review interface definitions for Telemetry and Tasking to ensure completeness.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and associated DPs is needed to fully cover all productivity aspects and reduce coupling.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Enable effective task scheduling and coordination

revise.why: To decompose productivity KPI into more granular functional requirements for comprehensive coverage.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Network link supports required communication bandwidth and latency.
open_risks: Interface complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Potential performance impact due to network latency.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data quality and availability reports.
Network performance metrics during representative operations.
